description: Shared rule-engine vectors (M3). Both the Java relay engine and the Swift VigilaCore engine must pass every case. Expected events/state are derived from SPEC §5 and docs/contracts/m3-rule-engine-design.md §§1-5. Timestamps are fixed ISO-8601 UTC instants (the engine is pure; now is injected). Loaders MUST ignore unknown top-level keys (schemaVersion, coverageIndex).

cases.name: link_state_fires_after_debounce
cases.description: running=false on two consecutive fast ticks fires on the second (D=2, H=0).

cases.name: link_state_debounce_interrupted
cases.description: true then false resets trueCount, emits nothing, informationless OK key prunes.

cases.name: link_state_resolves
cases.description: FIRING->RESOLVED emits resolved, state returns to OK, key pruned after 60 s.

cases.name: link_state_direction_both_up_always_notifies
cases.description: direction:both forces the up-transition notification even with notifyRecovery:false.

cases.name: link_state_multiple_ifaces_independent
cases.description: Two subjects keep independent keys; firing order equals params order.

cases.name: router_unreachable_fires_on_third_failure
cases.description: Counters 1 and 2 emit nothing; the third failed fast poll fires (effective D=1).

events.subject: router
events.title: rtr-brno-core unreachable
events.body: rtr-brno-core · router · 3 consecutive fast polls failed

cases.name: router_unreachable_resolves_on_success
cases.description: One successful fast poll (counter 0) resolves with no recovery debounce.

events.subject: router
events.title: rtr-brno-core unreachable
events.body: rtr-brno-core · router · 3 consecutive fast polls failed

cases.name: router_unreachable_freezes_snapshot_rules
cases.description: Failed fast ticks evaluate only router_unreachable; a FIRING link_state key is frozen untouched.

events.subject: router
events.title: rtr-brno-core unreachable
events.body: rtr-brno-core · router · 3 consecutive fast polls failed

cases.name: netwatch_down_fires_and_resolves
cases.description: Full netwatch lifecycle; subject is the host string.

cases.name: netwatch_empty_hosts_watches_all
cases.description: hosts:[] enumerates every snapshot netwatch entry in snapshot order.

cases.name: netwatch_unknown_status_fires
cases.description: status 'unknown' counts as not-up and fires.

cases.name: netwatch_disabled_entry_resolves
cases.description: Disabling a firing netwatch entry makes the predicate false and resolves it.

cases.name: cpu_sustained_requires_hold_duration
cases.description: trueCount reaches D but the y-minute hold is unmet, so the key stays PENDING.

cases.name: cpu_sustained_fires_after_hold
cases.description: Fires once both debounce count and the y-minute hold are satisfied; body shows current load.

cases.name: cpu_dip_resets_hold
cases.description: One sample at or below x resets both trueCount and pendingSince.

cases.name: cpu_sustained_resolves
cases.description: A FIRING cpu incident resolves on the first sample at or below x.

cases.name: temp_threshold_per_sensor_subjects
cases.description: Each temperature sensor is an independent subject; non-temperature kinds are ignored.

cases.name: temp_threshold_fires_and_resolves
cases.description: Strict > fires; the boundary value x itself resolves (<=x).

cases.name: mem_low_boundary_exact_percent_ok
cases.description: Exactly x% free does not fire (free*100 == x*total, predicate is strict <).

cases.name: mem_low_fires_and_resolves
cases.description: Integer-math predicate fires; body pct uses Int64 floor division; resolves at >=x.

events.subject: memory
events.title: Memory recovered on rtr-brno-core
events.body: rtr-brno-core · memory · 15% free, at or above 10%

cases.name: reboot_detected_first_sample_no_baseline
cases.description: First normal sample has a null baseline: no event, baseline stored.

cases.name: reboot_detected_pulse
cases.description: An uptime drop fires a pulse; phase stays ok, baseline updates, no resolved event ever.

events.subject: uptime
events.title: rtr-brno-core rebooted
events.body: rtr-brno-core · uptime · uptime dropped from 788705s to 120s

cases.name: pppoe_drop_fires_on_window_max_reference
cases.description: Reference is the window maximum; strict Int64 predicate (ref-cur)*100 > x*ref.

events.subject: pppoe
events.title: PPPoE sessions dropped on rtr-brno-core
events.body: rtr-brno-core · pppoe · 80 active, down 20% from 100 in 5 min

cases.name: pppoe_drop_window_expiry_resolves
cases.description: The depressed level ages out of the y-minute window, becomes the new reference, predicate false, resolves.

events.subject: pppoe
events.title: PPPoE sessions dropped on rtr-brno-core
events.body: rtr-brno-core · pppoe · 80 active, down 20% from 100 in 5 min

cases.name: pppoe_drop_zero_reference_never_fires
cases.description: An all-idle concentrator (ref==0) is never a drop.

cases.name: wg_null_handshake_is_stale
cases.description: A null last-handshake fires; subject is name when present else publicKey.

cases.name: wg_handshake_fires_and_resolves
cases.description: last-handshake > x*60 (strict) fires; a disabled peer is not enumerated; boundary x*60 resolves.

events.subject: peer-a
events.title: WireGuard peer peer-a stale
events.body: rtr-brno-core · peer-a · last handshake 6 min ago

events.subject: peer-a
events.title: WireGuard peer peer-a active
events.body: rtr-brno-core · peer-a · last handshake 5 min ago

cases.name: already_true_at_first_evaluation_fires
cases.description: Level semantics at cold start: a host already down at first evaluation fires (D=1).

cases.name: notify_recovery_false_suppresses_resolved
cases.description: notifyRecovery:false makes the RESOLVED transition happen with no resolved event.

cases.name: flap_suppression_defers_within_60s
cases.description: A second notification less than 60 s after the first is deferred, not emitted.

cases.name: flap_deferred_firing_emits_after_window
cases.description: A firing deferred by the flap gate emits on a later tick with ts = emission now.

cases.name: flap_pair_cancellation
cases.description: A firing that was deferred (never told) then resolves before emission: emit nothing, deferred cleared.

cases.name: resend_after_interval_still_firing
cases.description: While still FIRING, a resend after resendAfterMin emits a firing event with a recomposed body and sets lastResendAt.

events.subject: router
events.title: rtr-brno-core unreachable
events.body: rtr-brno-core · router · 3 consecutive fast polls failed

events.subject: router
events.title: rtr-brno-core unreachable
events.body: rtr-brno-core · router · 4 consecutive fast polls failed

cases.name: resend_zero_disables
cases.description: resendAfterMin:0 never resends, however long the incident stays FIRING.

cases.name: resend_suppressed_while_acknowledged
cases.description: acknowledged suppresses resends; the RESOLVED transition still notifies and clears acknowledged.

cases.name: quiet_hours_suppresses_warning
cases.description: A warning firing inside the quiet-hours window is deferred (Europe/Prague, winter UTC+1).

cases.name: quiet_hours_critical_bypasses
cases.description: A critical firing bypasses quiet hours and emits inside the window.

cases.name: quiet_hours_boundary_emits_deferred
cases.description: When the quiet-hours window ends, the deferred firing emits (condition still true) with ts = emission now.

cases.name: quiet_hours_overnight_window
cases.description: Wrap-around window 22:00-07:00: local 23:30 and 06:30 suppress; local 12:00 emits. notifyRecovery:false keeps the between-incident resolves silent (pair-cancellation).

cases.name: quiet_hours_timezone_conversion
cases.description: UTC now 21:30 is outside 22:00-23:00 but Europe/Prague local 22:30 is inside, so the warning is deferred (proves tz conversion of injected now).

cases.name: quiet_hours_unknown_timezone_fails_open
cases.description: An unknown IANA zone fails open: quiet hours are ignored, the event emits, and a diagnostic is produced.

cases.name: star_router_rule_applies
cases.description: A routerId:"*" rule materializes keys and dedupKeys with the concrete router id.

events.subject: uptime
events.title: rtr-brno-core rebooted
events.body: rtr-brno-core · uptime · uptime dropped from 788645s to 100s

cases.name: unknown_rule_type_skipped_with_diagnostic
cases.description: An unknown rule type is skipped: no events, no keys, exactly one diagnostic (forward compatibility).

cases.name: subject_disappears_while_firing_freezes_then_prunes
cases.description: A missing subject freezes the FIRING key (missingStreak++); at missingStreak>=60 the key prunes silently with no resolved event.

cases.name: restart_state_reload_no_respam
cases.description: A reloaded FIRING+notified key produces no event for the same condition; a resend fires only when resendAfterMin elapses.

cases.name: reboot_pulse_deferred_by_quiet_hours_emits_at_boundary
cases.description: A warning reboot pulse suppressed inside quiet hours defers with phase ok and emits when the window ends (design §4.7 + §3.5). Pins Java<->Swift parity on the suppressed-pulse deferral path (the §5.3 matrix had no suppressed-pulse case).

events.subject: uptime
events.title: rtr-brno-core rebooted
events.body: rtr-brno-core · uptime · uptime dropped from 50s to 50s

cases.name: cpu_wrong_typed_params_skips_rule
cases.description: A wrong-typed param (x given as a string) skips the rule with an invalid_params diagnostic instead of silently falling back to a default (design §2.1). Pins Java<->Swift parity on malformed params; reachable from the YAML seed path where a quoted scalar becomes a string.

cases.name: quiet_hours_unknown_timezone_no_diagnostic_when_silent
cases.description: A rule with an unresolvable IANA zone emits NO unknown_timezone diagnostic on a tick where no notification is wanted (design §3.6): the diagnostic accompanies a wanted notification only. Pins Java<->Swift parity on lazy diagnostic timing (the diagnostic fires in quiet_hours_unknown_timezone_fails_open, where a firing IS wanted).

cases.name: frozen_rule_key_survives_prune_on_foreign_tick
cases.description: A prune-eligible informationless-OK cpu_sustained key is frozen (NOT pruned) on a fast tick because cpu_sustained evaluates only on normal ticks (design §2.4); the §2.2 informationless-OK prune still fires on its own normal tick. Pins the Java↔Swift prune-scope parity: frozen keys pass through unchanged on a foreign tick.

cases.name: unknown_rule_type_retains_existing_state_frozen
cases.description: An enabled rule whose type an older relay does not recognize is skipped with a diagnostic, but its pre-existing incident state is retained frozen (design §2.1/§3.9 — unknown ≠ removed), so a relay/app version skew never wipes incident state. Pins the Java↔Swift unknown-type retention parity.
